compiled_state.title: onboarding: worktree.post_create_cmd runs in $REPO_ROOT instead of $WORKTREE_PATH
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Actual Behavior

When a host project sets `worktree.post_create_cmd=npm install` (as generated by /dso:onboarding), the command runs in $REPO_ROOT rather than the newly-created worktree directory. This causes the dependency install to target the wrong directory — the main repo instead of the isolated worktree.

## Expected Behavior

`worktree.post_create_cmd` should execute in $WORKTREE_PATH (the new worktree directory). Commands like `npm install` need to run where the new worktree is checked out.

## Evidence

Host project log:
> The hook runs from $REPO_ROOT but the worktree needs npm install in $WORKTREE_PATH. The config already has worktree.post_create_cmd=npm install but it runs in the wrong directory.

Host project fix applied:
```
-worktree.post_create_cmd=npm install
+worktree.post_create_cmd=cd "$WORKTREE_PATH" && npm install
```

## Root Cause (Suspected)

Two issues may be present:
1. The hook dispatcher that runs `worktree.post_create_cmd` does not `cd` into $WORKTREE_PATH before executing the command.
2. The /dso:onboarding skill generates `worktree.post_create_cmd=npm install` without the $WORKTREE_PATH prefix, giving no indication to users that the CWD at execution time is $REPO_ROOT.

## Remediation Options

- Fix the hook dispatcher to set CWD=$WORKTREE_PATH before running the command (preferred — least surprise, no burden on users).
- OR update onboarding-generated config to always prefix with `cd "$WORKTREE_PATH" &&` and document the CWD behavior.
